title: Routing
description: File-based routing, layouts, and SPA navigation

blocks.text: Page files live in client/pages/. The file path maps directly to the URL.
A page component receives ctx as its argument, which is the full request context including state, params, url, and all response helpers.
Wrap a segment name in brackets to make it dynamic. Access captured values via ctx.params.
Use [...slug] for catch-all routes that match any depth.
A _layout.tsx file wraps all pages in its directory and below. Layouts receive a Component prop that renders the child page.
Layouts nest — pages/docs/_layout.tsx applies only to /docs/* routes and stacks on top of pages/_layout.tsx.
Layouts can be async, letting you fetch data for the shell (e.g., navigation items from a database) before the page renders.
_app.tsx provides the outer HTML shell — the <html>, <head>, and <body> tags. Every page is rendered inside it.
The f-client-nav attribute enables SPA navigation. Without it, every link click triggers a full page reload.
When f-client-nav is set on <body>, Howl intercepts link clicks and fetches only the <Partial> region that changed — no full page reloads.
Navigation triggers a GET request with a special header. The server detects this and returns only the partial HTML fragment, which the client swaps into the DOM.
Partials are identified by name. Wrap any region you want to update independently:
Average SPA navigation in Howl takes 5-10ms on a local server. The single-pass SSR render and Link preload headers keep it fast.
